description: Implement receipt PDF generation, Stripe payment integration, Socket.io real-time sync, auto-updater config, offline resilience, error handling, and seed data. Complete all remaining backend services and integration points.
steps: Create src/main/services/receipt.ts - PDF generation with pdfkit: business logo, name, address, order number, date/time, itemized list, discounts, tax breakdown, total, payment method, change due, cashier name, thank-you message. Support 80mm thermal receipt format
Create src/main/services/stripe.ts - Stripe payment processing: create payment intent, confirm payment, handle errors with retry (3x with exponential backoff), timeout (10s)
Create src/main/services/scheduler.ts - node-cron scheduled tasks: daily profit summary calculation at midnight, writes to daily_summary table
Create src/main/services/socket.ts - Socket.io server setup for real-time sync between terminals: broadcast order events, stock updates, product changes
Create src/main/services/backup.ts - auto-backup service: copy SQLite database file to configured backup folder on schedule
Create src/main/services/updater.ts - electron-updater configuration pointing to GitHub Releases, check for updates on app start, notify user of available updates
Create src/main/routes/payments.ts - POST /api/payments/process (Stripe card payment), POST /api/payments/cash (record cash payment), handle split payments
Create src/main/routes/receipts.ts - POST /api/receipts/generate (create PDF, return file path or base64)
Create src/renderer/hooks/useSocket.ts - Socket.io client hook for receiving real-time updates
Create src/renderer/hooks/useOffline.ts - detect online/offline status, show 'AI unavailable' banner when offline, fall back to cash-only mode if Stripe unreachable
Create src/renderer/components/shared/OfflineBanner.tsx - styled banner showing offline status and degraded features
Create src/renderer/components/shared/ErrorBoundary.tsx - React error boundary with user-friendly error display
Create src/main/middleware/errorHandler.ts - Express error handling middleware with user-friendly messages and error logging
Create src/main/middleware/validation.ts - Zod schema validation for all IPC channels and API requests
Update src/main/main.ts - add auto-save cart timer (every 30s), register all IPC handlers with Zod validation, configure nodeIntegration:false and contextIsolation:true

Create prisma/seed.ts - seed script with 20 sample products (matching categories: Food, Drink, Dessert, Retail, Combo, Electronics, Clothing), a default manager user (PIN: 1234), and default settings (tax rate 16%, business name)
Add seed script to package.json prisma configuration
Create src/main/services/security.ts - encrypted storage for API keys using electron-store with encryption, bcrypt for PIN hashing
Ensure all API calls have timeout (10s) + retry (3x with exponential backoff) pattern
Ensure AI JSON parsing is wrapped in try/catch with fallback to manual entry mode
Ensure database errors show user-friendly messages and write to an error log file
acceptance_criteria: Receipt PDF generation creates a properly formatted PDF with all required fields
Stripe integration processes payments and handles errors gracefully with retry logic
Socket.io broadcasts order and stock update events
node-cron daily summary job is configured correctly
Offline detection works and UI degrades gracefully (AI unavailable banner, cash-only mode)
All API endpoints validate input with Zod schemas
Seed script creates 20 products and default manager user
Auto-updater is configured to check GitHub Releases
Error handling covers all API calls, AI parsing, and database operations
npx tsc --noEmit passes for all new files
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/POS-AI && npm run build 2>&1 | tail -20
Run: cd /projects/sandbox/POS-AI && npx prisma generate && node -e "require('./prisma/seed.ts')" 2>&1 | head -20
findings: All services created and integrated. tsc --noEmit passes with 0 errors. npm run build completes successfully. Prisma generate works. The existing useSocket.ts hook in src/renderer/hooks/ was already implemented in FEAT-003, so it was left as-is. The Socket.io service in src/main/services/socket.ts provides additional helper functions for broadcasting but the server.ts already had inline Socket.io setup which was preserved.
